novel_features.name: Cross-Recipe Ingredient Search
novel_features.description: Find every recipe that uses a given ingredient instantly — no scrolling, no guessing.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a join across the local recipes and ingredients tables that only exists after a SQLite sync; the live API has no ingredient-based search endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to suggest meals from available pantry items without making multiple API calls.

novel_features.name: Recipe Filter by Metadata
novel_features.description: Filter your entire recipe library by prep time, rating, serving count, and collection simultaneously.
novel_features.rationale: Requires querying multiple PBRecipe fields (prep_time, rating, servings, collection membership) across the full library — only possible with local SQLite; the app offers only name search.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to find recipes that fit a time or quality constraint for meal planning.

novel_features.name: Store-Partitioned Shopping Output
novel_features.description: Split a shopping list into per-store groups sorted by store aisle order — ready for multi-store shopping trips.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining items.store_ids → stores.sort_index from the local SQLite cache; the app shows items per list but never groups them by store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to generate a structured shopping route across multiple stores.

novel_features.name: Cron-Safe Meal-Plan-to-List
novel_features.description: Automatically build this week's shopping list from your meal plan — idempotent, quantity-merging, and safe to run on a schedule.
novel_features.rationale: Requires merging ingredient quantities across multiple recipes and writing idempotently via /data/shopping-lists/update; no existing tool has --dry-run or idempotency guarantees.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this in a cron job or agent workflow to pre-populate the shopping list before a weekly grocery trip.

novel_features.name: Recipe Add-to-List with Quantity Merge
novel_features.description: Add a recipe's ingredients to your list and automatically sum quantities when an ingredient is already present.
novel_features.rationale: Reads current list state from local cache and computes arithmetic merges (e.g. 1 tbsp + 2 tbsp = 3 tbsp) before writing; no existing tool merges quantities.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when adding multiple recipes to avoid duplicate line items with fractional quantities that confuse shoppers.

novel_features.name: Missing-Ingredients Check
novel_features.description: See exactly which ingredients you still need to buy before adding a recipe — skip what's already on your list.
novel_features.rationale: Computes the set difference between recipe ingredients and current unchecked list items using a local SQLite join; no API call needed, and no existing tool does this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before adding a recipe to a list to avoid redundant items and show users only the net-new ingredients they need.

novel_features.name: Meal Plan Weekly Grid
novel_features.description: Render a Mon–Sun meal plan grid with Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner labels — pasteable into messages or scripts.
novel_features.rationale: Joins meal_plan_events + calendar_labels + recipe names in local SQLite to render a structured grid; no existing CLI or MCP tool renders a terminal calendar view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to present the week's meal plan in a human-readable format for sharing or review.

novel_features.name: Weekly List Reset
novel_features.description: Clear all checked items from a list in one command — idempotent and safe for cron to run after every shopping trip.
novel_features.rationale: Issues a batched PbListOperationList update to uncheck (or remove) all checked items at once; no existing tool has a bulk reset with --keep-unchecked semantics.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this in a post-trip automation to reset the list for the next week without manually unchecking each item.

novel_features.name: Cross-List Item Search
novel_features.description: Find any item across all your shopping lists at once — shows which list it's on and whether it's checked.
novel_features.rationale: Queries the items table across all list_ids in local SQLite; every other tool (app, MCP, npm) scopes search to a single named list.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to locate an item without knowing which list it was added to.

novel_features.name: Sync Cache Staleness Check
novel_features.description: Report how fresh your local cache is per entity type, with exit code 1 if any data is stale.
novel_features.rationale: Reads _sync_meta table timestamps; the exit-code contract enables cron and CI scripts to fail fast before operating on stale data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this as a cron preflight check to ensure agent operations run against up-to-date local data.

narrative.headline: Every AnyList feature in your terminal — plus offline search, store routing, and cron-safe automations no mobile app can match.
narrative.value_prop: anylist-pp-cli syncs your shopping lists, recipes, and meal plan to a local SQLite database, then lets you query and automate everything from the shell. Search recipes by ingredient, split shopping lists by store, and build this week's grocery list from your meal plan — all scriptable, JSON-outputting, and safe to run on a schedule.
narrative.auth_narrative: AnyList uses email + password authentication returning a short-lived access_token and a refresh_token. The CLI stores these in ~/.config/anylist-pp-cli/config.toml and transparently refreshes on 401 responses. All requests require two additional headers: X-AnyLeaf-API-Version: 3 and a stable X-AnyLeaf-Client-Identifier (a 32-char hex UUID generated once per device and reused).

quickstart.comment: Authenticate with your AnyList email and password
Pull all your lists, recipes, and meal plan into the local SQLite cache
Pipe unchecked items to jq for scripting
View this week's meal plan as a Mon-Sun grid
Preview adding this week's recipe ingredients to your grocery list

troubleshoots.fix: Run `anylist-pp-cli auth refresh` to force a token refresh, or `auth login` to re-authenticate

troubleshoots.symptom: Commands return stale data (old item names, missing lists)
troubleshoots.fix: Run `anylist-pp-cli sync --force` to pull the latest state from the server
Run `anylist-pp-cli sync` first — ingredient search queries the local cache which must be populated
The client identifier in ~/.config/anylist-pp-cli/config.toml must be a stable 32-char hex string; delete the config and re-run `auth login` to regenerate

narrative.when_to_use: Use anylist-pp-cli when you need to automate grocery and meal planning workflows from the shell or in agent pipelines. It excels at cron-based shopping list generation from meal plans, offline recipe search by ingredient or metadata, and store-optimized shopping output. It is the right choice when you need JSON output from AnyList operations for downstream processing with jq, or when you want to build Home Assistant / n8n automations beyond what the basic hacs-anylist integration supports.

recipes.title: Sunday meal-prep: build grocery list from this week's plan
recipes.command: anylist-pp-cli meal add-to-list --week --list Groceries --merge --dry-run && anylist-pp-cli meal add-to-list --week --list Groceries --merge
recipes.explanation: Preview then execute: merges ingredient quantities from all this week's meal plan events into the Groceries list, idempotently.

recipes.title: Find quick weeknight recipes with available chicken
recipes.command: anylist-pp-cli recipe search --ingredient chicken --agent | anylist-pp-cli recipe filter --max-prep 30 --min-rating 4
recipes.explanation: Search by ingredient then filter by metadata — pure offline SQLite, no API calls.

recipes.title: Check what you still need before adding a recipe
recipes.command: anylist-pp-cli recipe missing "Thai Green Curry" --list Groceries --agent
recipes.explanation: Shows only the ingredients not already on your shopping list — prevents duplicates.

recipes.title: Get per-store shopping route as structured JSON
recipes.explanation: Groups items by store with sort_index ordering; --select narrows the payload for agent context.

recipes.title: Post-trip cleanup + next-week reset via cron
recipes.command: anylist-pp-cli list reset Groceries --keep-unchecked && anylist-pp-cli sync status --max-age 12h || anylist-pp-cli sync
recipes.explanation: Remove checked items, keep unchecked, then ensure cache is fresh — safe to run in a cron job.

trigger_phrases: what recipes can I make with
build my shopping list from meal plan
